commit.message:
Pixi support (#24)

* Add pixi and nix flake configuration for local development

Add pixi.toml with conda-forge dependencies (Python 3.10, PyTorch, scanpy,
geneformer, etc.) and pip editable install of cellwhisperer + cellxgene fork.
Include flake.nix providing a NixOS FHS environment to run pixi.

* Update cellxgene submodule: Werkzeug 3.x fix, pandas 2.x, rename to cellxgene-cellwhisperer

Points to epigen/cellxgene commit 90345e3 which includes:
- Fix FlatBuffers bytes/bytearray compatibility with Werkzeug 3.x
- Require pandas>=2.0.1
- Rename package to cellxgene-cellwhisperer to avoid conflicts
- Skip Puppeteer download in client Makefile

* Fix torch.load compatibility with PyTorch 2.6+

PyTorch 2.6 changed torch.load default to weights_only=True, which rejects
checkpoints containing custom classes (e.g. TranscriptomeTextDualEncoderConfig).
Pass weights_only=False explicitly where needed, and monkey-patch torch.load in
load_from_checkpoint to handle Lightning checkpoint loading.

* Make scgpt imports lazy to support environments without scgpt

Move scgpt imports inside functions that use them so the package can run with
geneformer (default) without requiring scgpt to be installed. Also use string
comparison for foundation model class checks to avoid import dependency.

* Add pixi installation instructions to README

Add pixi as the top-most (recommended) installation option, keeping the
existing conda instructions as Option B.

* Fix missing gseapy in pixi

* Replace local LLaVA/Mixtral with API + lightweight local curation

- llava_annotate_clusters now uses the hosted CellWhisperer API instead of
  requiring a local LLaVA model and separate conda env
- Unified curate_llava_annotations rule replaces separate gpt4/mixtral rules:
  uses OpenAI GPT-4 if OPENAI_API_KEY is valid, falls back to local
  Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct via transformers
- Add cellwhisperer_api_url to config.yaml (used by API annotation rule)
- Upgrade transformers>=4.45 and add accelerate for local model support
- Add gseapy dependency

* Retain local LLaVA annotation rule as llava_annotate_clusters_local

API-based rule is preferred via ruleorder. The local rule can be
invoked explicitly with --forcerun llava_annotate_clusters_local
and requires the llava conda env + GPU.

* Update README with pixi commands and new curation fallback docs

* Add news section to landing page (Pixi support, Mila talk)

* Revert "Add news section to landing page (Pixi support, Mila talk)"

This reverts commit 76f405e4d7a21f793a1993e96c6d96cf4d97cfbd.

* Fix checkpoint loading for newer transformers and add frontend build step to README

* Simplify Pixi setup: remove Nix files, drop uni dep, add setup script (#25)

* Initial plan

* Address review comments: remove flake files, remove uni dep, simplify README with setup script

commit.message:
iccanalyzer-lite: enhance H17 MCS validation, H23 NaN CWE from wontfix issues

H17 — Spectral Range: add icMaterialColorSignature (MCS) enum validation.
Invalid MCS values (not 0 and not 0x6d63xxxx 'mc' prefix) trigger UBSAN
"load of value N, which is not a valid value for type" in
CIccCmm::AddXform() (iccDEV #323, CWE-843). Also validates MCS channel
count range (1-32) and reports deviceSubClass when non-zero.

H23 — NumArray Value Range: add CWE-681 reference to NaN/Inf detection.
NaN values propagate through MPE/Spectral/CLUT paths and cause UB at
IccIO.cpp:298 WriteUInt16Float() when scaling to uint16 (iccDEV #536).

Reviewed 9 closed wontfix issues from iccDEV:
- #536 NaN outside range in IccIO.cpp:298 → enhanced H23
- #323 Invalid icMaterialColorSignature enum → enhanced H17
- #525 Null CIccApplyCmm (fuzzer-only, OOS) → no action needed
- #353 SEGV CIccCmm::Apply null m_pApply (fuzzer-only, OOS) → documented
- #570 Version workflow (non-security) → N/A
- #310 CodeQL config (CI) → N/A
- #195 CLA vpkg (admin) → N/A
- #137 OBS binaries (admin) → N/A

All 172 tests pass. Zero ASAN/UBSAN errors on spot checks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

host resolution, and proxy env context.

fix(webui): handle YYYY-MM-DD date format from folder-based todos

The new folder-based TODO structure returns due dates in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM
format, but the WebUI only parsed MM/DD/YYYY HH:MM:SS. This caused a
TypeError (undefined.padStart) inside the map callback, which triggered

commit.message:
Fix humidity_index scale semantics in get_ams_units and get_ams_status docstrings

Veil-of-ignorance smoke test finding: naive agent saw humidity_index=5 on
both AMS units and recommended drying, because no scale context was provided.

The Bambu firmware scale is counterintuitive: 1=WET (alert), 5=DRY (good).
Without this documented in the tool docstrings, any agent reading the raw
value will invert the meaning and give incorrect advice.

Also documents that 0=unavailable/uninitialized and must not be treated as wet.

commit.message:
Phase 3 Step 9: First Triangle Demonstration - GPU command submission infrastructure

Implements the first GPU command submission demonstration binary, validating
the complete Phase 3 GPU submission infrastructure from Go to GPU.

## What Changed

### New Demonstration Binary
- Created cmd/gpu-triangle-demo/main.go (~430 LOC)
  - Demonstrates end-to-end GPU command submission workflow
  - GPU detection via render.DetectGPU()
  - GPU context creation via render.CreateContext()
  - Batch buffer allocation and command construction
  - Batch submission via render.SubmitBatch() with synchronous wait
  - GPU buffer presentation to X11 window via DRI3/Present extensions

### Go API Extensions
- Added BufferHandle.GemHandle() method to internal/render/dmabuf.go
  - Exposes GEM buffer handle for use in GPU command submission
  - Required for batch buffer and render target references

### Rust C ABI Extension
- Added buffer_get_handle() export in render-sys/src/lib.rs
  - Returns the GEM handle from a Buffer pointer
  - Called by Go's BufferHandle.GemHandle() via CGO

### Build System
- Updated Makefile with gpu-triangle-demo target
  - Uses same static linking configuration as other demos
  - Added to .PHONY targets list

## Phase 3 Achievement

This demo completes the GPU command submission infrastructure validation:
✅ GPU hardware detection (Gen9/Gen11/Gen12/Xe)
✅ GPU context creation (i915 and Xe drivers)
✅ Batch buffer allocation and construction
✅ GPU command submission with relocations
✅ Synchronous completion wait
✅ GPU buffer sharing with X11 display server

## Current Limitations (Phase 4 Scope)

The demo submits a minimal batch (MI_NOOP + MI_BATCH_BUFFER_END) as an
infrastructure smoke test. Full triangle rendering requires:
- Shader compilation (GLSL/WGSL → Intel EU binary) - Phase 4
- CPU→GPU buffer copy for batch data upload
- Render target surface state and pipeline configuration
- Vertex buffer setup and 3DPRIMITIVE command emission

The infrastructure is complete; the next phase will add the rendering pipeline.

## Validation Results

- All 100 Rust tests passing
- All 17 Go package tests passing
- Static linking verified: "not a dynamic executable"
- Zero regressions detected
- 4 complexity improvements in unrelated code (shm.HandleEvent, widget-demo)
- Binary builds successfully on x86_64 musl target

## Files Modified

- Makefile: Added gpu-triangle-demo build target
- PLAN.md: Marked Step 9 as complete with implementation notes
- cmd/gpu-triangle-demo/main.go: New demonstration binary (430 LOC)
- internal/render/dmabuf.go: Added GemHandle() method
- render-sys/src/lib.rs: Added buffer_get_handle() C ABI export
- baseline.json, post.json: Updated metrics (auto-generated)

Next: Step 10 - Integration Test Suite

commit.message:
feat: implement destructuring and spread support in bytecode generator

Destructuring support:
- Variable declarations: let {a, b} = obj; let [x, y] = arr;
- Catch parameters: catch ({message}) { ... }
- Assignment targets: [a, b] = expr; ({x, y} = expr)
- For-in loops: for (let {length} in obj) { ... }
- For-of loops: for (let [k, v] of entries) { ... }

All locations wire through the existing compile_binding_pattern() which
already handles object patterns, array patterns, nested patterns, and
default values.

Spread support:
- Array spread: [...arr, x, ...other] with dynamic index counter
- Object spread: {...obj, key: val} via new CopyDataProperties opcode
- Expr::Spread compiles its argument for use by parent contexts

New opcode: CopyDataProperties [target_reg, source_reg] — copies all
own enumerable properties from source to target PlainObject.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
